stem: Sarah works $38$ hours in a week and is paid $\$24.50$ per hour. Calculate her weekly wage.
hint: Weekly wage $=$ hours worked $\times$ hourly rate.

stem: James earns $\$18.40$ per hour and works $35$ hours in a week. Calculate his weekly wage.
hint: Weekly wage $=$ hours worked $\times$ hourly rate.

stem: A cashier is paid $\$21.60$ per hour for a $38$-hour week. What is her weekly pay?
hint: Multiply the hourly rate by the number of hours worked.

stem: Marcus earns $\$16.50$ per hour and works $40$ hours per week. Find his weekly wage.
hint: Weekly wage $=$ hours worked $\times$ hourly rate.

stem: Tom earns $\$22.00$ per hour for a standard $38$-hour week. He works $4$ hours overtime at time-and-a-half. What is his total weekly pay?
hint: Calculate standard pay and overtime pay separately, then add. The overtime rate is $1.5 \times$ the normal rate.
explanation:
Standard pay: $38 \times \$22.00 = \$836.00$

Overtime rate: $\$22.00 \times 1.5 = \$33.00$ per hour

Overtime pay: $4 \times \$33.00 = \$132.00$

$$\text{Total pay} = \$836.00 + \$132.00 = \$968.00$$

stem: Lisa is paid $\$26.00$ per hour for a standard $38$-hour week. She works $3$ additional hours at double time. What is her gross weekly pay?
hint: The double-time rate is $2 \times$ the normal rate. Calculate each component separately.
explanation:
Standard pay: $38 \times \$26.00 = \$988.00$

Double-time rate: $\$26.00 \times 2 = \$52.00$ per hour

Overtime pay: $3 \times \$52.00 = \$156.00$

$$\text{Total pay} = \$988.00 + \$156.00 = \$1144.00$$

stem: Michael earns $\$19.80$ per hour. He works $38$ standard hours and $5$ hours overtime at time-and-a-half. Calculate his weekly pay.
hint: Time-and-a-half rate $= 1.5 \times$ normal rate. Calculate standard and overtime pay then add.
explanation:
Standard pay: $38 \times \$19.80 = \$752.40$

Overtime rate: $\$19.80 \times 1.5 = \$29.70$ per hour

Overtime pay: $5 \times \$29.70 = \$148.50$

$$\text{Total pay} = \$752.40 + \$148.50 = \$900.90$$

stem: Rachel earns $\$24.00$ per hour. Her employer pays double time for Saturday work. She works $38$ standard hours and $4$ hours on Saturday. Find her total weekly pay.
hint: Saturday double-time rate $= 2 \times \$24.00$. Calculate weekday and Saturday pay separately.
explanation:
Standard pay: $38 \times \$24.00 = \$912.00$

Saturday rate: $\$24.00 \times 2 = \$48.00$ per hour

Saturday pay: $4 \times \$48.00 = \$192.00$

$$\text{Total pay} = \$912.00 + \$192.00 = \$1104.00$$

stem: Priya works $76$ standard hours in a fortnight at $\$28.50$ per hour. She also works $6$ hours on Saturday at time-and-a-half and $3$ hours on a public holiday at double time. Calculate her fortnightly gross income.
hint: Calculate three components: standard pay, Saturday pay (rate $\times 1.5$) and public holiday pay (rate $\times 2$), then add.
explanation:
Standard pay: $76 \times \$28.50 = \$2166.00$

Saturday rate: $\$28.50 \times 1.5 = \$42.75$ per hour

Saturday pay: $6 \times \$42.75 = \$256.50$

Public holiday rate: $\$28.50 \times 2 = \$57.00$ per hour

Public holiday pay: $3 \times \$57.00 = \$171.00$

$$\text{Fortnightly gross} = \$2166.00 + \$256.50 + \$171.00 = \$2593.50$$

stem: Dan earns $\$32.00$ per hour for a standard $38$-hour week. In a fortnight he works his standard hours, plus $8$ hours overtime at time-and-a-half and $4$ hours on a public holiday at double-time-and-a-half. Find his fortnightly gross income.
hint: Double-time-and-a-half rate $= 2.5 \times$ base rate. Calculate all three pay components then sum.
explanation:
Standard pay: $76 \times \$32.00 = \$2432.00$

Time-and-a-half rate: $\$32.00 \times 1.5 = \$48.00$ per hour

Overtime pay: $8 \times \$48.00 = \$384.00$

Double-time-and-a-half rate: $\$32.00 \times 2.5 = \$80.00$ per hour

Public holiday pay: $4 \times \$80.00 = \$320.00$

$$\text{Fortnightly gross} = \$2432.00 + \$384.00 + \$320.00 = \$3136.00$$

stem: Amy works $76$ standard hours per fortnight at $\$21.50$ per hour. She also works $5$ hours on Saturday at time-and-a-half and $2$ hours on Sunday at double time. Calculate her fortnightly gross income.
hint: Calculate standard pay, Saturday pay (rate $\times 1.5$) and Sunday pay (rate $\times 2$) separately, then add.
explanation:
Standard pay: $76 \times \$21.50 = \$1634.00$

Saturday rate: $\$21.50 \times 1.5 = \$32.25$ per hour

Saturday pay: $5 \times \$32.25 = \$161.25$

Sunday rate: $\$21.50 \times 2 = \$43.00$ per hour

Sunday pay: $2 \times \$43.00 = \$86.00$

$$\text{Fortnightly gross} = \$1634.00 + \$161.25 + \$86.00 = \$1881.25$$

stem: A worker earns $\$27.60$ per hour for a standard $38$-hour week. In one fortnight they work their standard hours plus $10$ hours overtime: the first $6$ hours at time-and-a-half and the remaining $4$ hours at double time. Calculate their fortnightly gross income.
hint: There are three pay components: standard hours, the first block of overtime (rate $\times 1.5$) and the second block (rate $\times 2$).
explanation:
Standard pay: $76 \times \$27.60 = \$2097.60$

Time-and-a-half rate: $\$27.60 \times 1.5 = \$41.40$ per hour

First overtime block: $6 \times \$41.40 = \$248.40$

Double-time rate: $\$27.60 \times 2 = \$55.20$ per hour

Second overtime block: $4 \times \$55.20 = \$220.80$

$$\text{Fortnightly gross} = \$2097.60 + \$248.40 + \$220.80 = \$2566.80$$
